# Wispr

> Wispr is a browser-based **cursor trail designer** (mouse pointer effects) for websites—not the Cursor IDE. Design trails in a live playground, then export JavaScript, CSS, HTML, React, Vue, or Angular snippets.

## Canonical site

- Production: https://wispr.dev/

## Main pages

- https://wispr.dev/ — Marketing home, FAQ, links to playground and guides.
- https://wispr.dev/playground.html — Interactive designer: presets, shapes, colors, chase/lag, blend, glow, multi-tab export.
- https://wispr.dev/guides/ — Guides index + **Playground features** list (`#features`).
- https://wispr.dev/guides/cursor-trail-effect-css.html — Vanilla JS / CSS / HTML embed.
- https://wispr.dev/guides/react-cursor-trail-component.html — React export integration.
- https://wispr.dev/guides/vue-angular-cursor-trail.html — Vue 3 and Angular exports.
- https://wispr.dev/support.html — Contact and troubleshooting.

## Content ops

- Weekly guide workflow (exports → guides → sitemap): https://wispr.dev/guides/publishing-loop.md

## Machine-readable discovery

- Sitemap: https://wispr.dev/sitemap.xml
- Robots: https://wispr.dev/robots.txt

## Analytics (build-time)

- Production builds inject GA4 and Google Search Console verification when `VITE_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` and `VITE_GOOGLE_SITE_VERIFICATION` are set (see `.env.example`). After deploy, submit the sitemap in Search Console and use URL Inspection for priority URLs; optional IndexNow via `npm run seo:launch`.

## Keywords (intent)

cursor trail designer, cursor trail generator, CSS cursor trail, mouse trail effect, React Vue Angular cursor trail export.
